study: SG-WF-004 taddhita residue — -in/-ika/-ya via MW POS + base-attest + DCS
method: MW headwords ending in the suffix that MW marks adjectival (mfn/m:f#iṇī) = possessive/relational signature (POS-grounded); base-attested subset requires the vṛddhi-reversed stem to be an attested MW headword. Joined to pinned DCS for tokens.

corpus_snapshot.note: pin 04e0778 bound by provenance + tag c3-pin-04e0778-content

limits.ya_polyfunctional: -ya overlaps kṛt gerundive; the adjectival+base-attested filter is the defence, sample measures leakage
limits.pos_grounded_vs_base: POS-grounded is broader (any MW adjectival -X); base-attested is the transparent-derivation lower bound
limits.compounds_included: compound-final -in/-ya (aṃśa-bhāgin) are counted — they carry the suffix; not separated from simplex here
